retry_reason: Enable failures (e.g., unsupported notification) indicate a permanent condition; retrying would not succeed. Disable is unconditional and never fails.
contract_summary: This function manages per-source notification event reference counts. On enable, it increments the refcount (or initializes from 0 to 1) and triggers hardware enable on the first reference; on error (e.g., unsupported) it returns a negative errno. The caller must ensure src_id is valid and pair every successful enable with a disable to release the reference. The disable path decrements the refcount and triggers hardware disable on the last reference. The caller must not call disable on a source that has not been successfully enabled (or already disabled). The function returns 0 on success (at least one source handled) and negative errno on enable failure.

judge.reason: The function's primary purpose is to manage per-source notification event refcounts with automatic hardware enable/disable on first/last reference. It contains minimal non-refcount logic (range check and error counting).

check.reason: All three operations (refcount_set, refcount_inc, refcount_dec_and_test) are invoked on r_evt->sources[src_id] in the function. The access path from parameter 1 through .sources[] and .refs is consistent with the callee's path (r.refs). The type chain scmi_registered_event->refcount_t->atomic_t matches the actual struct definitions. Segment counts match.
